R1.emotional_beat: A sober click of certainty: the ‘accident’ framing finally dies when the 23:12 nitrogen purge and pre-impact hypoxia line up on one timeline.

detective_annotations.content: This sentence ends the ‘routine fall’ theory from A1 — officially homicide now.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow highlight with a tight digital callout box

detective_annotations.content: Anchor time: 23:12 purge (C2). Use this to sanity-check every alibi claim.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red circle; timestamp underlined twice

R1.reading_order_note: Read this first in Envelope R. It sets the final, evidence-anchored timeline (C1 + C2) before you evaluate the POI disposition memos.

R2.emotional_beat: Relief mixed with irritation: Kline had the sleazy motive and corporate evasions, but the method evidence won’t stay pinned to him once the shared credential loophole is stated plainly.

detective_annotations.content: Key takeaway: EXEC-0441 is a record, not a person. Shared credential ≠ physical presence.
Still… his ‘EXEC-0441’ slip in B1 felt too fast. Not murder—maybe optics/obstruction.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small gray text box; cautious tone

R2.reading_order_note: This plays best after R1. The emotional release depends on the player remembering how suspicious B1 felt before B5/C2 reframed it.

R3.emotional_beat: A gentler kind of closure: Reyes is guilty of fear-driven ‘paper sins,’ but the 22:48–23:17 engineered window makes it clear she’s not the one who turned the building into a weapon.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Paper liar’ vs. murderer — her audit panic explains B2, not C2.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow highlight; short note pinned to the margin
tight circle with an arrow pointing to ‘engineered-event window’

R3.reading_order_note: Read after R2 if you want a clean ‘suspect triage’ feeling: finance motive cleared (R2), then messy-but-human lab motive cleared (R3).

R4.emotional_beat: Uncomfortable accountability: Briggs isn’t the killer, but his ‘this is normal’ security culture reads like the open door the offender walked through.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Phone never rang’ (B3) isn’t an alibi when MAINT/SILENT exists (C2 @ 22:48).
Policy fix: escort/override after-hours needs written approval + auto-expire. B5 shouldn’t be this ambiguous.

R4.reading_order_note: This memo lands hardest if read right before R5: it clarifies how the system could be exploited, then R5 shows who did exploit it.

R5.emotional_beat: Catharsis with a chill: the motive (DEV-102-17 and audit-trail exposure) and the method (silence + purge + export) converge into a timestamp chain that feels too engineered to deny.

detective_annotations.style_hint: green highlight; bracketed timeline in the margin

detective_annotations.content: B7 (DEV-102-17 panic) → C2/C3 (RPATEL session) = motive meets method.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin black arrow with labeled endpoints

detective_annotations.content: The cover-story move is the 23:36 ‘sensor fault’ export. That’s consciousness of guilt, not confusion.
detective_annotations.style_hint: clean digital callout; ‘why it matters’ tone

R5.reading_order_note: Read this last in Envelope R. The payoff depends on having R1’s homicide determination and the cleared-POI logic (R2–R4) already in your head.
